title: The ExoContact Sedona Conference
summary: The conference the record's own pandemic swallowed — promoted, anticipated, and cancelled across three consecutive sessions of spring 2020.
markdown:
**The ExoContact Sedona Conference** is a small entry with a sharp irony: it is the piece
of the record's own history that the record's central subject consumed.

Across three consecutive [James Rink](/lore/james-rink/) interviews the event rises and
falls in real time. In late February 2020, [Maxwell Scott](/lore/maxwell-scott/) is
introduced as the man *"who will be hosting both Kosol and myself at the ExoContact event
in Sedona, Arizona, March 21 & 22, 2020."* By mid-March the plan is still alive — Kosol
is *"going to be speaking about your [Source-Coil Device](/lore/source-coil-device/)"* at
the *"Sedona Conference Workshop"* — and by March 26 it is gone: *"Thank you, for
everybody who was planning on coming to Sedona; obviously they cancelled the event."*

The sessions those weeks were devoted to [Operation Lusterkill](/lore/operation-lusterkill/)
— the record explaining the pandemic — while the pandemic quietly cancelled the record's
own gathering. ExoContact Sedona never happened; the archive preserves it anyway, the way
archives do: as the shape of a plan the timeline declined.
